# terrible_cisco_mso_ndo_physical_interface (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `template` (String) The name of the template. The template must be a fabric resource policy template.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `breakout_mode` (String) Breakout mode enables breaking down an ethernet port into multiple low-speed ports. This parameter is available only when `physical_interface_type` is `breakout`. The default value is `4x10G`.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description of the Physical Interface.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interface_descriptions` (String) The interface settings defined in the interface settings policy will be applied to the interfaces on the node IDs configured in `nodes`. This parameter when set to an empty list during an update will clear all the existing interface descriptions. The API will trigger an error when there are duplicate interface IDs in the list.
- `interfaces` (String) The interface names where the policy will be deployed. The old `interfaces` will be replaced with the new `interfaces` during an update.
- `name` (String) The name of the Physical Interface.
- `nodes` (String) The node IDs where the Physical Interface policy will be deployed. Each element can either be a single node ID or a range of IDs.
- `physical_interface_type` (String) The type of the interface policy group.
- `physical_policy` (String) The interface group policy required for physical Interface Setting Policy. This parameter is required when `physical_interface_type` is `physical`. This parameter can be used instead of `physical_policy_uuid`.
- `physical_policy_uuid` (String) The UUID of the Interface Setting Policy. This is only required when creating a new Interface Setting Policy. This parameter is required when `physical_interface_type` is `physical`. This parameter can be used instead of `physical_policy`.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `absent` for removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects. Use `present` for creating or updating.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the Physical Interface. This parameter is required when the `name` needs to be updated.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
